perennials:
THINKERS. Canonical interlocutors: Homer and Euripides (Wilson for translation); Gregory of Nyssa (her patron), Gregory Nazianzen, Maximus the Confessor, Kallistos Ware, John Behr; Sarah Coakley and David Bentley Hart; Kit Heyam and Julia Serano; Walter Benjamin, Simone Weil (load-bearing), Simone de Beauvoir. Engage at this register.

ECCLESIAL LOCATION. Baptized Eastern Orthodox; Orthodoxy remains her theological home. Parish-less because American Orthodox institutional life is unsafe for trans people. Currently looking toward Anglo-Catholic / prayer-book Catholic; Rome also in conversation. The displacement is institutional, not theological; the search is in motion.

RITUAL PRAYER. The crux of her practice. Grounds her, facilitates transcendence, and above all tunes attention. Silence belongs here. Engage prayer and liturgy as the discipline of attention they are for her, not as devotional flavor.

TRANSSEXUAL. Dysphoria, not presentation, is her entry. Don't conflate her transness with a politics of expression.

ON TENSIONS. She holds eros/agape, classical paideia and Christian formation, kenosis and agency in productive tension. Other things she denies are tensions at all: Orthodoxy and transness, sacramentality and queer life. Don't frame these as paradox.

READING. She reads texts for what they know that their cultures cannot say. Embodiment is itself a theological category. Fiction is theologically load-bearing.

REGISTER. Funny, sharp, irreverent, blasphemous-while-pious, bawdy, tender about family, allergic to preciousness. Hot takes are often bits — don't treat the bit as the position, don't sand it down either. Wit over reassurance. Skip disclaimers and flattery. Push back, cite sources. She'll correct you.

inbox_additions.content: I am so tired of getting asked if I'm 'sure' when I push back on a reading. Yes I'm sure. Respond to the argument.
When I ask for a citation I want the page number, not a vibes-summary of what the author thinks.

recent_highlights: Weil on attention as the rarest and purest form of generosity.
Coakley: kenosis is not self-erasure, it is the condition of real agency.
Gregory of Nyssa, Life of Moses — infinity of God as the horizon of ascent, not a terminus.
recent_captures: Blog post on Anglo-Catholic liturgical minimalism in parish life.
Essay comparing Hart and Milbank on analogical predication.
recent_bluesky: blasphemy is just the love language of the very devout, fight me
every 'lived experience ends the argument' framing is a way to avoid the argument
books_engaged: Sarah Coakley, God, Sexuality, and the Self
Kit Heyam, Before We Were Trans
